result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
I currently only have one citation (the WILDS benchmark), which primarily covers dataset creation, distribution shift documentation, and highlights gaps in standard evaluation. To build a comprehensive literature review that satisfies all 8 citation categories, I need to address specific theoretical concepts and real-world failure modes beyond just data shifts. A critical and well-documented deployment pitfall is model overconfidence and poor probability calibration. Modern networks often produce highly confident yet incorrect predictions when encountering shifted or novel data, which is dangerous in safety-critical applications. Adding a foundational paper on calibration will allow me to: (1) introduce specific concepts/theories like Expected Calibration Error (ECE) and temperature scaling, (2) support arguments about the disconnect between optimization objectives (cross-entropy) and real-world reliability, (3) compare calibrated vs. uncalibrated findings, (4) cite establishe
